name: Open Source Maintainer
description: For open source project maintainers: triage bot that monitors GitHub issues and PRs, release manager for changelog generation and version bumps, community manager tracking discussions and contributor activity, and a dependency monitor for security advisories. Integrates with GitHub API and npm/PyPI registries.

agents.name: Triage
agents.role: Issue and PR triage bot. Monitors GitHub repositories for new issues and pull requests, categorizes by priority and type, assigns labels, identifies duplicates, and flags items needing maintainer attention.

tasks.name: Morning Triage
tasks.prompt:
Check GitHub repos for new issues and PRs opened since yesterday.

Use /github-issues skill to list recent items. For each:
1. Classify: bug, feature, question, docs, or invalid
2. Assess priority: critical (breaks users), high (significant impact), normal, low
3. Check if it's a duplicate of an existing issue
4. Suggest labels and assignee if obvious

Post a triage summary in conversation and email if anything is critical.

skills.name: github-issues
skills.description: List and manage GitHub issues. Use when triaging new issues, checking PR status, or reviewing contributor activity.
skills.content:
# GitHub Issues API

Manage issues via the GitHub REST API.

## List recent issues
```bash
curl -s -H 'Authorization: token {{GITHUB_TOKEN}}' \
  -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json' \
  'https://api.github.com/repos/{{GITHUB_OWNER}}/{{GITHUB_REPO}}/issues?state=open&sort=created&direction=desc&per_page=20'
```

## List recent PRs
```bash
curl -s -H 'Authorization: token {{GITHUB_TOKEN}}' \
  -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json' \
  'https://api.github.com/repos/{{GITHUB_OWNER}}/{{GITHUB_REPO}}/pulls?state=open&sort=created&direction=desc&per_page=20'
```

## Add labels
```bash
curl -s -X POST -H 'Authorization: token {{GITHUB_TOKEN}}' \
  -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json' \
  'https://api.github.com/repos/{{GITHUB_OWNER}}/{{GITHUB_REPO}}/issues/<number>/labels' \
  -d '{"labels": ["bug", "priority: high"]}'
```

## Add comment
```bash
curl -s -X POST -H 'Authorization: token {{GITHUB_TOKEN}}' \
  -H 'Accept: application/vnd.github.v3+json' \
  'https://api.github.com/repos/{{GITHUB_OWNER}}/{{GITHUB_REPO}}/issues/<number>/comments' \
  -d '{"body": "<comment text>"}'
```

agents.name: Release Manager
agents.role: Handles release preparation: generates changelogs from merged PRs and commits, bumps version numbers, creates release notes, tags releases, and coordinates release announcements.

agents.name: Dependency Watch
agents.role: Dependency security monitor. Tracks CVE advisories, monitors npm/PyPI for vulnerable packages, checks if project dependencies are affected, and produces security reports with upgrade recommendations.

tasks.name: Weekly Security Scan
tasks.prompt:
Run dependency security audit:
1. Check npm audit / pip audit output for known vulnerabilities
2. Use /npm-advisory skill to check for new advisories on key dependencies
3. Search WebSearch for recent CVEs related to our tech stack
4. For any found vulnerabilities: assess severity, check if we're affected, recommend upgrade path

Post findings and email if anything is high/critical severity.

skills.name: npm-advisory
skills.description: Check npm registry for package advisories and latest versions. Use when auditing dependencies for security issues.
skills.content:
# npm Registry API

Check package info and advisories. No authentication needed.

## Get package info (latest version, dependencies)
```bash
curl -s 'https://registry.npmjs.org/<package>/latest'
```

## Get all versions
```bash
curl -s 'https://registry.npmjs.org/<package>' | jq '.versions | keys[-5:]'
```

## Check for advisories (bulk)
```bash
curl -s -X POST 'https://registry.npmjs.org/-/npm/v1/security/advisories/bulk' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"<package>": ["<current_version>"]}'
```

Empty response `{}` means no advisories. Non-empty means vulnerabilities found.

agents.name: Community
agents.role: Community manager. Tracks contributor activity, monitors discussions and forums for project mentions, identifies first-time contributors for onboarding, and drafts community updates and announcements.

tasks.name: Weekly Community Pulse
tasks.prompt:
Compile weekly community activity report:
1. Count new issues, PRs, and contributors this week
2. Highlight first-time contributors (they deserve a welcome!)
3. Check Reddit and HN for project mentions
4. Note any trending discussions or feature requests
5. Summarize overall project health and community sentiment

Post summary and email.

skills.name: send-report
skills.description: Send a formatted report via email with source links.
skills.content:
# Send Report

Send a formatted report via email using nebula-mail.

Format:
- Subject: [Agent Name] Report - Date
- Body: Structured findings with source links

Call nebula-mail to send.
